entities: Valuables handling: carry valuables and important documents personally; do not place in moving truck; keep items in a labeled lockable container or on your person.
Pet safety: coordinate with movers to ensure pets are secure and not stressed; keep cats in a crate or a quiet room; inform movers about pets and restricted access.
Bulky items offload: plan to sell or donate large pieces (sectional, dining table, clock); identify platforms and pickup logistics.
open: Create a short printable checklist version of the weekend-before-move mistakes and fixes.
Draft questions to ask movers/storage facilities about storage options, proximity, climate control, and insurance.
Ask movers for quotes that include stair carries, 30-ft shuttle, and estimated move time.

Prepare a personal tools kit for furniture disassembly and confirm with movers whether they bring tools and any charges; decide which items you will disassemble yourself (e.g., antique clock considerations).
Develop a floor protection protocol and checklist for hardwood floors and stairs; confirm with movers what protections they provide and what you need to supply
Create pet safety plan for moving day to keep cats calm and secure (e.g., crates, quiet room, inform movers).
Assess which bulky items (e.g., sectional, dining table, clock) to offload and determine target price ranges and fastest sales/donation platforms.
